extracted_concepts.name: Higher-Order Derivatives
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Derivatives of derivatives: f''(x), f'''(x), f⁽⁴⁾(x), etc.

extracted_concepts.name: second derivative
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: The derivative of the derivative: f''(x) = d²f/dx²

extracted_concepts.name: acceleration
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: The second derivative of position: a(t) = s''(t) = v'(t)

extracted_concepts.name: jerk
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: The third derivative of position: s'''(t)

extracted_relations.evidence: The Product Rule gives the derivative of a product of functions
The Quotient Rule can be derived from the Product Rule and Chain Rule
The Quotient Rule gives the derivative of a ratio of functions
Higher-order derivatives generalize the concept of first derivative
The second derivative is the first higher-order derivative
Acceleration is the second derivative of the position function
Jerk is the third derivative of position
